Feature:AMO001 Get Member Wallet Balance 191ms 5

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant wallet balance API
So that I can retrieve the member wallet balance for one or more currencies

Scenario:
Get balances for requested currencies
46ms 4

Given a merchant member exists 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info 

Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: 44ms
field value
platform_username <platform_username>
currencies ["CNY","THB"]
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY",
            "THB"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 30.625,
                "THB": 0
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Info 

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY",
            "THB"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 30.625,
                "THB": 0
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Then the AMO001 response should be successful < 1ms

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: 15ms
field value

currencies ["CNY","THB"]
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY",
            "THB"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "platform_username",
                "message": "\"platform_username\" is not allowed to be empty"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: 67ms
field value
platform_username <platform_username>
currencies ["USD","MYR","INVALID"]
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "USD",
            "MYR",
            "INVALID"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "currencies.1",
                "message": "Each currency must be one of: CNY,VND,USDT,THB,TRY,INR,AUD,PHP,BRL,MXN,NPR,BDT,IDR,KRW,JPY,HKD,PKR,RON,DKK,NOK,MMK,TWD,CUP,USD,EUR,GBP,CHF,CAD,RUB,KES,KKC,LUCK,MYX"
              },
              {
                "key": "currencies.2",
                "message": "Each currency must be one of: CNY,VND,USDT,THB,TRY,INR,AUD,PHP,BRL,MXN,NPR,BDT,IDR,KRW,JPY,HKD,PKR,RON,DKK,NOK,MMK,TWD,CUP,USD,EUR,GBP,CHF,CAD,RUB,KES,KKC,LUCK,MYX"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Feature:AMO013 Notify Wager Update < 1ms 3

As APISYS
I want to notify the merchant of wager updates

Scenario:
Successful wager notification
< 1ms 2

When APISYS notifies wager update with: < 1ms 

{
  "notification_type": "typeA",
  "notifications": [
    {
      "game_type": "IMSPORT",
      "game_key": "IMSPORT",
      "wager_no": "0001-20250117140015-188011927396-3",
      "origin_wager_no": "0001-20250117140015-188011927396-1",
      "ticket_no": "ticketno_123",
      "platform_username": "member01",
      "type": 3,
      "status": 2,
      "currency": "CNY",
      "amount": 10.1,
      "payment_amount": 10.1,
      "effective_amount": 10,
      "profit_and_loss": 5.1,
      "wager_time": 1720694402,
      "settlement_time": 1720698600,
      "is_system_reward": true
    },
    {
      "game_type": "CRASH",
      "game_key": "CRASH_V2",
      "wager_no": "0002-20250117140015-188011927398-1",
      "origin_wager_no": null,
      "ticket_no": null,
      "platform_username": "member02",
      "type": 1,
      "status": 2,
      "currency": "CNY",
      "amount": 5.1,
      "payment_amount": 5.1,
      "effective_amount": 5,
      "profit_and_loss": 0,
      "wager_time": 1720694403,
      "settlement_time": null,
      "is_system_reward": false
    }
  ]
}
 

Then(/^APISYS notifies wager update with:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Then the AMO013 response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the AMO013 response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant request payment API
So that I can deduct wager payment from the member wallet

Scenario:

59ms 5 2 1

Given a merchant member exists 1ms Show Info +

Info

        supermerchant1user

Given the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>" 29ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {

              "balances": {

        }

Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-payment
    Payload:
        {

field value
parent_wager_no <parent_wager_no>
payment_order_no <payment_order_no>

  callback(null, 'pending');
});

parent_wager_no <parent_wager_no>

Feature:AMO007 Seamless Settle Wager 60ms 4

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant settle wager API
So that I can apply wager settlement results to the member wallet

Scenario:
Full settlement credits the wallet
22ms 2 2 2

Given a merchant member exists < 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info

    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

          ]
        }
    Response:

          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 30.625

            "message": "Success"

        }

    platform_username:

        supermerchant1user

Given I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>" 20ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 30.625
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

    CNY:
        30.625

    Payload:

        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 105.75
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

    Currency:

    Before:
        30.625
    After:
        105.75
    Expected increase:
        75.125

field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount 50
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-in
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "1c63440a-70f9-406c-b42a-e4d7bb31ea0b",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "50",
          "session_id": "56693928-9313-4486-841e-465b2da3a4c8"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "02e757cf-bce5-4736-ad2a-9a9c51c05678",
              "status": 1
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Then the AMO010 response should be successful < 1ms

And the response should contain: 4ms
field value
reference_id any non-empty value
status 1

And I store the response field "reference_id" as "amo010_reference_id" < 1ms

And the wallet balance in "<currency>" should increase by 50 17ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:

          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }

        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 155.75
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

When APISYS requests transfer in with: 12ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount 1.1234567
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-in
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "f161353f-0a53-41b7-bb31-2d863b506f1f",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "1.1234567",
          "session_id": "01067661-c653-4cd7-9840-c725f1a9e4d7"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "amount",
                "message": "Amount must be a number with maximum 6 decimal places"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Info

    API:

        {
          "transfer_no": "dac04fde-f89d-4535-86fe-51c9e19fbaf6",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "amount": "10",
          "session_id": "7f05a386-361f-44ca-822a-d4d50c5bc1d3"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "currency",
                "message": "Currency is required"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Then the AMO010 response should fail validation < 1ms

Feature:AMO011 Request Transfer Out 456ms 7 2 2

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant transfer out API
So that I can debit funds from the member wallet

Scenario:
Transfer out decreases wallet balance
77ms 6 1

Given a merchant member exists < 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info 

Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Given the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>" 14ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 175.75
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }
Balance:
    CNY:
        175.75

Info 

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 175.75
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }
Balance:
    CNY:
        175.75

When APISYS requests transfer out with: 41ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -20.5
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "d3488486-019f-401c-9170-598e05f78d65",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-20.5",
          "session_id": "bd7fc2ed-d1b3-4ed5-bbb4-068e19895f31"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "af686752-b4f7-4496-b1c8-2e39e4b18155",
              "amount": -20.5,
              "status": 1
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Info 

Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "d3488486-019f-401c-9170-598e05f78d65",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-20.5",
          "session_id": "bd7fc2ed-d1b3-4ed5-bbb4-068e19895f31"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "af686752-b4f7-4496-b1c8-2e39e4b18155",
              "amount": -20.5,
              "status": 1
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Then the AMO011 response should be successful < 1ms

And the response should contain: < 1ms
field value
reference_id any non-empty value
amount -20.5
status 1

And the wallet balance in "<currency>" should decrease by 20.5 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance in "([^"]*)" should decrease by 20.5$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Repeating the same transfer_no is idempotent
73ms 7 1 5

When APISYS requests transfer out with: 30ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -15
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "07d69c9e-0cfe-4437-807d-be5b5cb9b8b5",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-15",
          "session_id": "b9edabae-d2c5-495c-b2bd-c22bdef95b44"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "5eee9c51-070d-42e6-a3af-17365e9103f1",
              "amount": -15,
              "status": 1
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Info 

Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "07d69c9e-0cfe-4437-807d-be5b5cb9b8b5",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-15",
          "session_id": "b9edabae-d2c5-495c-b2bd-c22bdef95b44"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "5eee9c51-070d-42e6-a3af-17365e9103f1",
              "amount": -15,
              "status": 1
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Then the AMO011 response should be successful < 1ms

And the response should contain: < 1ms
field value
reference_id any non-empty value
amount -15
status 1

And I store the response field "reference_id" as "amo011_reference_id" < 1ms

And the wallet balance in "<currency>" should decrease by 15 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance in "([^"]*)" should decrease by 15$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Given I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>" < 1ms

When APISYS requests transfer out with: < 1ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -15
session_id <session_id>

Then the AMO011 response should be successful < 1ms

And the response should contain: < 1ms
field value
reference_id <amo011_reference_id>
amount -15
status 1

And the wallet balance in "<currency>" should remain unchanged < 1ms

Scenario:
Insufficient balance returns failed status
73ms 7

Given a merchant member exists < 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Given I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>" 18ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }

        {
          "status": 200,

            "code": 200,

              "balances": {
                "CNY": 140.25

Info 

Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 140.25
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }
Balance:
    CNY:
        140.25

    balance:

game_type <game_type>

    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "e1b5e4a6-bd39-4612-a19c-64c9807023a8",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-150.25",
          "session_id": "64c649f2-ae4d-40aa-8767-48dc402f9250"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "reference_id": "e1b5e4a6-bd39-4612-a19c-64c9807023a8",
              "amount": 0,
              "status": 2
            },
            "message": "Success"

Request:
    API:

    Payload:

          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 0.25
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

When APISYS requests transfer out with: 12ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>
game_type <game_type>
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount -1.1234567
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "044674d4-2615-4350-899e-b01b83192db7",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currency": "CNY",
          "amount": "-1.1234567",
          "session_id": "62d80eda-9572-47d2-b534-c15d22b99b8a"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "amount",
                "message": "Amount must be a number with maximum 6 decimal places"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Info

Scenario:

field value
transfer_no <transfer_no>

amount -10
session_id <session_id>
Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        POST http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/request-transfer-out
    Payload:
        {
          "transfer_no": "d4f93d02-456a-4f6d-840e-2049addae36e",
          "game_type": "",
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "amount": "-10",
          "session_id": "963761d8-7bb0-42f7-86ca-6e26a9b19f92"
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 400,
          "body": {
            "error": [
              {
                "key": "currency",
                "message": "Currency is required"
              }
            ],
            "message": "Parameter error",
            "code": 400,
            "message_code": 11028
          }
        }

Feature:AMO014 Request Cancel Transfer 20ms 3

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant cancel transfer API
So that I can reverse a previous transfer ledger

Scenario:
Cancel transfer returns reference_id for an existing transfer in and is idempotent
< 1ms 1 5 3

Given a merchant member exists < 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info 

Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Given a successful transfer in exists for: < 1ms
field value
platform_username <platform_username>
transfer_no <transfer_no>
currency <currency>
amount 25 

Then(/^a successful transfer in exists for:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests cancel transfer with: < 1ms
field value
transfer_no <transfer_no> 

Then(/^APISYS requests cancel transfer with:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Then the AMO014 response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the AMO014 response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain: < 1ms
field value
reference_id any non-empty value

And I store the response field "reference_id" as "cancel_reference_id" < 1ms

field value
transfer_no <transfer_no> 

Then(/^APISYS requests cancel transfer with:$/, function(callback) {

  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');

field value
reference_id <cancel_reference_id>

Given the member has positive wallet balance in "<currency>" 19ms Show Info + 
Request:
    API:
        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:

          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 0.25
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

Feature:AMO014 Cancel transfer integration flow 87ms 1

As APISYS
I want to cancel an actual existing transfer
So that repeated cancel calls behave consistently

Scenario:
Transfer in then cancel twice returns the same reference_id
87ms 5 4 4

Given a merchant member exists < 1ms Show Info + 
Setup:
    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Info

    platform_username:
        supermerchant1user

Given I record the current wallet balance in "<currency>" 21ms Show Info +

        GET http://localhost:4000/api/v1/transfer-wallet/balance
    Payload:
        {
          "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
          "currencies": [
            "CNY"
          ]
        }
    Response:
        {
          "status": 200,
          "body": {
            "code": 200,
            "data": {
              "platform_username": "supermerchant1user",
              "balances": {
                "CNY": 0.25
              }
            },
            "message": "Success"
          }
        }

    CNY:
        0.25

Feature:Integration Flow - Transfer out all integer balance < 1ms 1

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant transfer APIs in sequence
So that I can verify full integer-balance withdrawal behavior end to end

Scenario:
Transfer in 120.5 then transfer out without amount deducts 120 and leaves 0.5
< 1ms 7 4

Given a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>" < 1ms 

Then(/^a merchant member exists with platform username "([^"]*)"$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests transfer in with: < 1ms
transfer_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>
amount 120.5

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 120.5 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance for "([^"]*)" in "([^"]*)" should become 120.5$/, function(arg1, arg2, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests transfer out with: < 1ms
transfer_no
platform_username <platform_username>
currency <currency>

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain: < 1ms
status success
amount 120

And the wallet balance for "<platform_username>" in "<currency>" should become 0.5 < 1ms 

Then(/^the wallet balance for "([^"]*)" in "([^"]*)" should become 0.5$/, function(arg1, arg2, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

When APISYS requests member wallet balances with: < 1ms
platform_username
currencies <currency>

Then the response should be successful < 1ms 

Then(/^the response should be successful$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

And the response should contain balances: < 1ms
0.5 

Then(/^the response should contain balances:$/, function(callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions
  callback(null, 'pending');
});

Feature:Integration Flow - Transfer lifecycle through merchant wallet APIs < 1ms 1

As APISYS
I want to call the merchant wallet APIs in sequence
So that I can verify transfer credit, debit, reversal, and balance retrieval end to end

Scenario:
Transfer in 100, get balance, transfer out 35, get balance, cancel transfer, get balance
< 1ms 14 8

Given a merchant member exists with platform username "<platform_username>" < 1ms 

Then(/^a merchant member exists with platform username "([^"]*)"$/, function(arg1, callback) {
  // Write code here that turns the phrase above into concrete actions

  callback(null, 'pending');
});
